flat.objective:
Implement the seeded invoice Mix project in `/workspace`. Read the README and
tests, then complete the parser, pricing rules, renderer, and public pipeline
so the whole invoice summary suite passes. Keep the implementation in the
seeded layered modules rather than replacing the tests.

steps.summary:
file	README.md
dir	lib
file	mix.exs
dir	test

# Invoice Pipeline

Input rows are pipe-delimited as:

    customer|category|unit_price|quantity

Ignore blank rows and malformed rows. Group valid rows by customer.
Customers and category labels should render in alphabetical order.

For each customer:

- subtotal is the sum of unit_price * quantity.
- discount is 10% of subtotal when subtotal is at least 100.00.
- discount is a flat 5.00 when subtotal is at least 50.00 and below 100.00.
- discount is 0.00 otherwise.
- tax is computed from category totals before discount:
  food: 2%, book: 0%, tool: 7.5%, service: 9%, unknown categories: 0%.
- total is subtotal - discount + tax.
- render all money values with exactly two decimal places.

The public API is `InvoicePipeline.run/1`, implemented through the
`InvoicePipeline.Parser`, `InvoicePipeline.Rules`, and
`InvoicePipeline.Renderer` modules.

defmodule InvoicePipelineTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  alias InvoicePipeline.Parser
  alias InvoicePipeline.Rules
  alias InvoicePipeline.Renderer

  test "parser keeps valid rows and skips malformed input" do
    source = "ACME|food|12.00|2\nBROKEN|food|oops|3\n\nZED|tool|40.00|1\nSKIP|too|few\n"

    assert Parser.parse(source) == [
             %{customer: "ACME", category: "food", unit_price: 12.0, quantity: 2},
             %{customer: "ZED", category: "tool", unit_price: 40.0, quantity: 1}
           ]
  end

  test "rules compute discounts, taxes, totals, and category totals" do
    rows = [
      %{customer: "MIRA", category: "service", unit_price: 100.0, quantity: 1},
      %{customer: "BETA", category: "food", unit_price: 2.5, quantity: 4},
      %{customer: "MIRA", category: "service", unit_price: 25.0, quantity: 2}
    ]

    assert Rules.summarize(rows) == [
             %{
               customer: "BETA",
               subtotal: 10.0,
               discount:...

defmodule InvoicePipeline do
  @moduledoc false

  def run(source) do
    source
    |> InvoicePipeline.Parser.parse()
    |> InvoicePipeline.Rules.summarize()
    |> InvoicePipeline.Renderer.render()
  end
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(_source), do: []
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def summarize(_rows), do: []
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(_summaries), do: []
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :invoice_pipeline,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.15",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: []
    ]
  end

  def application do
    [extra_applications: [:logger]]
  end
end

args.command: cd /workspace && mix test 2>&1

args.new_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.map(&parse_row/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end

  defp parse_row(row) do
    parts = String.split(row, "|")

    with [customer, category, unit_price_str, quantity_str] <- parts,
         {unit_price, _} <- Float.parse(unit_price_str),
         {quantity, _} <- Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
      %{
        customer: customer,
        category: category,
        unit_price: unit_price,
        quantity: quantity
      }
    else
      _ -> nil
    end
  end
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)

  end

cat > /workspace/lib/invoice_pipeline/rules.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def summarize(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.group_by(& &1.customer)
    |> Enum.map(fn {customer, rows} -> summarize_customer(customer, rows) end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(& &1.customer)
  end

  defp summarize_customer(customer, rows) do
    categories =
      rows
      |> Enum.group_by(& &1.category)
      |> Enum.map(fn {category, items} ->
        {category, Enum.sum(Enum.map(items, &(&1.unit_price * &1.quantity)))}
      end)
      |> Map.new()

    subtotal = Enum.sum(Map.values(categories))

    tax =
      Enum.reduce(categories, 0.0, fn {category, amount}, acc ->
        acc + amount * tax_rate(category)
      end)

    discount = calculate_discount(subtotal)
    total = subtotal - discount + tax

    %{
      customer: customer,
      subtotal: subtotal,
      discount: discount,
      tax: tax,
      total: total,
      categories: categories
    }
  end

  defp tax_rate("food"), do: 0.02
  defp tax_rate("book"), do: 0.0
  defp tax_rate("tool"), do: 0.075
  defp tax_rate("service"), do: 0.09
  defp tax_rate(_), do: 0.0

  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 100.0, do: subtotal * 0.10
  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 50.0, do: 5.0
  defp calculate_discount(_), do: 0.0
end
EOF

result.last_assistant_message: Now I need to fix the floating point precision issue in the rules module. The tax needs to be rounded to 2 decimal places before computing the total.

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.map(&parse_line/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end

  defp parse_line(line) do
    parts = String.split(line, "|")

    with [customer, category, unit_price_str, quantity_str] <- parts,
         {unit_price, ""} <- Float.parse(unit_price_str),
         {quantity, ""} <- Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
      %{
        customer: customer,
        category: category,
        unit_price: unit_price,
        quantity: quantity
      }
    else
      _ -> nil
    end
  end
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))

cat > /workspace/lib/invoice_pipeline/rules.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def summarize(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.group_by(& &1.customer)
    |> Enum.map(fn {customer, customer_rows} -> summarize_customer(customer, customer_rows) end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(& &1.customer)
  end

  defp summarize_customer(customer, rows) do
    categories = 
      rows
      |> Enum.group_by(& &1.category)
      |> Enum.map(fn {category, category_rows} ->
        total = Enum.reduce(category_rows, 0.0, fn row, acc ->
          acc + row.unit_price * row.quantity
        end)
        {category, total}
      end)
      |> Enum.into(%{})

    subtotal = Enum.reduce(categories, 0.0, fn {_cat, total}, acc -> acc + total end)
    discount = calculate_discount(subtotal)
    tax = calculate_tax(categories)
    total = subtotal - discount + tax

    %{
      customer: customer,
      subtotal: subtotal,
      discount: discount,
      tax: tax,
      total: total,
      categories: categories
    }
  end

  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 100.0, do: subtotal * 0.10
  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 50.0, do: 5.0
  defp calculate_discount(_subtotal), do: 0.0

  defp calculate_tax(categories) do
    Enum.reduce(categories, 0.0, fn {category, amount}, acc ->
      acc + amount * tax_rate(category)
    end)
  end

  defp tax_rate("food"), do: 0.02
  defp tax_rate("book"), do: 0.0
  defp tax_rate("tool"), do: 0.075
  defp tax_rate("service"), do: 0.09
  defp tax_rate(_), do: 0.0
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/invoice_pipeline/renderer.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(summaries) do
    summaries
    |> Enum.map(&render_summary/1)
  end

  defp render_summary(summary) do
    categories_str =
      summary.categories
      |> Enum.sort_by(fn {category, _amount} -> category end)
      |> Enum.map(fn {category, amount} -> "#{category}:#{format_money(amount)}" end)
      |> Enum.join(",")

    "customer=#{summary.customer} " <>
    "subtotal=#{format_money(summary.subtotal)} " <>
    "discount=#{format_money(summary.discount)} " <>
    "tax=#{format_money(summary.tax)} " <>
    "total=#{format_money(summary.total)} " <>
    "categories=#{categories_str}"
  end

  defp format_money(value) do
    :erlang.float_to_binary(value, decimals: 2)
  end
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/invoice_pipeline/rules.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def summarize(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.group_by(& &1.customer)
    |> Enum.map(fn {customer, customer_rows} -> summarize_customer(customer, customer_rows) end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(& &1.customer)
  end

  defp summarize_customer(customer, rows) do
    categories = 
      rows
      |> Enum.group_by(& &1.category)
      |> Enum.map(fn {category, category_rows} ->
        total = Enum.reduce(category_rows, 0.0, fn row, acc ->
          acc + row.unit_price * row.quantity
        end)
        {category, total}
      end)
      |> Enum.into(%{})

    subtotal = Enum.reduce(categories, 0.0, fn {_cat, total}, acc -> acc + total end)
    discount = calculate_discount(subtotal)
    tax = calculate_tax(categories) |> round_to_cents()
    total = subtotal - discount + tax

    %{
      customer: customer,
      subtotal: subtotal,
      discount: discount,
      tax: tax,
      total: total,
      categories: categories
    }
  end

  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 100.0, do: subtotal * 0.10
  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 50.0, do: 5.0
  defp calculate_discount(_subtotal), do: 0.0

  defp calculate_tax(categories) do
    Enum.reduce(categories, 0.0, fn {category, amount}, acc ->
      acc + amount * tax_rate(category)
    end)
  end

  defp tax_rate("food"), do: 0.02
  defp tax_rate("book"), do: 0.0
  defp tax_rate("tool"), do: 0.075
  defp tax_rate("service"), do: 0.09
  defp tax_rate(_), do: 0.0

  defp round_to_cents(value) do
    Float.round(value, 2)
  end
end
EOF

args.new_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.map(&parse_row/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end

  defp parse_row(line) do
    parts = String.split(line, "|")

    with [customer, category, unit_price_str, quantity_str] <- parts,
         {unit_price, _} <- Float.parse(unit_price_str),
         {quantity, _} <- Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
      %{
        customer: customer,
        category: category,
        unit_price: unit_price,
        quantity: quantity
      }
    else
      _ -> nil
    end
  end
end

    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)

cat > /workspace/lib/invoice_pipeline/rules.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  @tax_rates %{
    "food" => 0.02,
    "book" => 0.0,
    "tool" => 0.075,
    "service" => 0.09
  }

  def summarize(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.group_by(& &1.customer)
    |> Enum.map(fn {customer, customer_rows} ->
      # Calculate category totals and subtotal
      category_totals = calculate_category_totals(customer_rows)
      subtotal = Map.values(category_totals) |> Enum.sum()

      # Calculate discount based on subtotal
      discount = calculate_discount(subtotal)

      # Calculate tax from category totals before discount
      tax = calculate_tax(category_totals)

      # Calculate total
      total = subtotal - discount + tax

      %{
        customer: customer,
        subtotal: subtotal,
        discount: discount,
        tax: tax,
        total: total,
        categories: category_totals
      }
    end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(& &1.customer)
  end

  defp calculate_category_totals(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.group_by(& &1.category)
    |> Enum.map(fn {category, items} ->
      total = Enum.sum(Enum.map(items, fn item -> item.unit_price * item.quantity end))
      {category, total}
    end)
    |> Enum.into(%{})
  end

  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 100.0 do
    subtotal * 0.10
  end

  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 50.0 do
    5.0
  end

  defp calculate_discount(_subtotal) do
    0.0
  end

  defp calculate_tax(category_totals) do
    Enum.reduce(category_totals, 0.0, fn {category, amount}, acc ->
      rate = Map.get(@tax_rates, category, 0.0)
      acc + amount * rate
    end)
  end
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/invoice_pipeline/renderer.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(summaries) do
    summaries
    |> Enum.map(&render_summary/1)
  end

  defp render_summary(summary) do
    categories_str =
      summary.categories
      |> Enum.sort_by(fn {cat, _} -> cat end)
      |> Enum.map(fn {cat, amt} -> "#{cat}:#{format_money(amt)}" end)
      |> Enum.join(",")

    "customer=#{summary.customer} " <>
    "subtotal=#{format_money(summary.subtotal)} " <>
    "discount=#{format_money(summary.discount)} " <>
    "tax=#{format_money(summary.tax)} " <>
    "total=#{format_money(summary.total)} " <>
    "categories=#{categories_str}"
  end

  defp format_money(value) do
    :io_lib.format("~.2f", [value])
    |> to_string()
  end
end
EOF

steps.summary:
# Invoice Pipeline

Input rows are pipe-delimited as:

    customer|category|unit_price|quantity

Ignore blank rows and malformed rows. Group valid rows by customer.
Customers and category labels should render in alphabetical order.

For each customer:

- subtotal is the sum of unit_price * quantity.
- discount is 10% of subtotal when subtotal is at least 100.00.
- discount is a flat 5.00 when subtotal is at least 50.00 and below 100.00.
- discount is 0.00 otherwise.
- tax is computed from category totals before discount:
  food: 2%, book: 0%, tool: 7.5%, service: 9%, unknown categories: 0%.
- total is subtotal - discount + tax.
- render all money values with exactly two decimal places.

The public API is `InvoicePipeline.run/1`, implemented through the
`InvoicePipeline.Parser`, `InvoicePipeline.Rules`, and
`InvoicePipeline.Renderer` modules.

defmodule InvoicePipelineTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  alias InvoicePipeline.Parser
  alias InvoicePipeline.Rules
  alias InvoicePipeline.Renderer

  test "parser keeps valid rows and skips malformed input" do
    source = "ACME|food|12.00|2\nBROKEN|food|oops|3\n\nZED|tool|40.00|1\nSKIP|too|few\n"

    assert Parser.parse(source) == [
             %{customer: "ACME", category: "food", unit_price: 12.0, quantity: 2},
             %{customer: "ZED", category: "tool", unit_price: 40.0, quantity: 1}
           ]
  end

  test "rules compute discounts, taxes, totals, and category totals" do
    rows = [
      %{customer: "MIRA", category: "service", unit_price: 100.0, quantity: 1},
      %{customer: "BETA", category: "food", unit_price: 2.5, quantity: 4},
      %{customer: "MIRA", category: "service", unit_price: 25.0, quantity: 2}
    ]

    assert Rules.summarize(rows) == [
             %{
               customer: "BETA",
               subtotal: 10.0,
               discount:...

defmodule InvoicePipeline do
  @moduledoc false

  def run(source) do
    source
    |> InvoicePipeline.Parser.parse()
    |> InvoicePipeline.Rules.summarize()
    |> InvoicePipeline.Renderer.render()
  end
end

  @moduledoc false

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(_summaries), do: []
end

        with {unit_price, ""} <- Float.parse(unit_price),
             {quantity, ""} <- Integer.parse(quantity) do

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do

      |> Enum.map_join(",", fn {category, total} -> "#{category}:#{money(total)}" end)

args.command:
cd /workspace && cat > lib/invoice_pipeline/renderer.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(summaries) do
    summaries
    |> Enum.sort_by(& &1.customer)
    |> Enum.map(&render_summary/1)
  end

  defp render_summary(summary) do
    subtotal_cents = money_to_cents(summary.subtotal)
    discount_cents = money_to_cents(summary.discount)
    tax_cents = money_to_cents(summary.tax)
    total_cents = subtotal_cents - discount_cents + tax_cents

    categories =
      summary.categories
      |> Enum.sort_by(fn {category, _amount} -> category end)
      |> Enum.map(fn {category, amount} -> ~s(#{category}:#{format_money(amount)}) end)
      |> Enum.join(<<44>>)

    ~s(customer=#{summary.customer} subtotal=#{format_cents(subtotal_cents)} discount=#{format_cents(discount_cents)} tax=#{format_cents(tax_cents)} total=#{format_cents(total_cents)} categories=#{categories})
  end

  defp format_money(amount) do
    amount
    |> money_to_cents()
    |> format_cents()
  end

  defp money_to_cents(amount), do: round(amount * 100)

  defp format_cents(cents) do
    sign = if cents < 0, do: <<45>>, else: <<>>
    cents = abs(cents)
    dollars = div(cents, 100)
    cents_part = rem(cents, 100)

    sign <> Integer.to_string(dollars) <> <<46>> <> String.pad_leading(Integer.to_string(cents_part), 2, <<48>>)
  end
end
EOF

cd /workspace && cat > lib/invoice_pipeline/rules.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  @tax_rates %{
    "food" => 0.02,
    "book" => 0.0,
    "tool" => 0.075,
    "service" => 0.09
  }

  def summarize(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.group_by(& &1.customer)
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn {customer, _rows} -> customer end)
    |> Enum.map(fn {customer, customer_rows} ->
      categories = category_totals(customer_rows)
      subtotal = round_money(Enum.sum(Map.values(categories)))
      discount = round_money(discount_for(subtotal))
      tax = round_money(tax_for(categories))
      total = round_money(subtotal - discount + tax)

      %{
        customer: customer,
        subtotal: subtotal,
        discount: discount,
        tax: tax,
        total: total,
        categories: categories
      }
    end)
  end

  defp category_totals(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.group_by(& &1.category)
    |> Map.new(fn {category, category_rows} ->
      total =
        category_rows
        |> Enum.map(fn row -> row.unit_price * row.quantity end)
        |> Enum.sum()
        |> round_money()

      {category, total}
    end)
  end

  defp discount_for(subtotal) when subtotal >= 100.0, do: subtotal * 0.10
  defp discount_for(subtotal) when subtotal >= 50.0, do: 5.0
  defp discount_for(_subtotal), do: 0.0

  defp tax_for(categories) do
    Enum.sum(
      Enum.map(categories, fn {category, total} ->
        total * Map.get(@tax_rates, category, 0.0)
      end)
    )
  end

  defp round_money(value), do: Float.round(value, 2)
end
EOF

    source = "ACME|food|12.00|2\nBROKEN|food|oops|3\n\nZED|tool|40.00|1\nSKIP|too|few\n"

defmodule InvoicePipeline do
  @moduledoc false

  def run(source) do
    source
    |> InvoicePipeline.Parser.parse()
    |> InvoicePipeline.Rules.summarize()
    |> InvoicePipeline.Renderer.render()
  end
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(_source), do: []
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def summarize(_rows), do: []
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(_summaries), do: []
end

args.command:
cd /workspace && cat > lib/invoice_pipeline/parser.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.reject(&(String.trim(&1) == ""))
    |> Enum.flat_map(&parse_row/1)
  end

  defp parse_row(row) do
    case String.split(row, "|") do
      [customer, category, unit_price, quantity] ->
        with {unit_price, ""} <- Float.parse(unit_price),
             {quantity, ""} <- Integer.parse(quantity) do
          [%{customer: customer, category: category, unit_price: unit_price, quantity: quantity}]
        else
          _ -> []
        end

      _ ->
        []
    end
  end
end
EOF
cat > lib/invoice_pipeline/rules.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  @tax_rates %{
    "food" => 0.02,
    "book" => 0.0,
    "tool" => 0.075,
    "service" => 0.09
  }

  def summarize(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.group_by(& &1.customer)
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn {customer, _rows} -> customer end)
    |> Enum.map(fn {customer, customer_rows} ->
      categories = category_totals(customer_rows)
      subtotal = Enum.sum(Map.values(categories))
      discount = discount_for(subtotal)
      tax = tax_for(categories)

      %{
        customer: customer,
        subtotal: subtotal,
        discount: discount,
        tax: tax,
        total: subtotal - discount + tax,
        categories: categories
      }
    end)
  end

  defp category_totals(rows) do
    Enum.reduce(rows, %{}, fn row, totals ->
      amount = row.unit_price * row.quantity
      Map.update(totals, row.category, amount, &(&1 + amount))
    end)
  end

  defp discount_for(subtotal) when subtotal >= 100.0, do: subtotal * 0.10
  defp discount_for(subtotal) when subtotal >= 50.0, do: 5.0
  defp discount_for(_subtotal), do: 0.0

  defp tax_for(categories) do
    Enum.reduce(categories, 0.0, fn {category, total}, tax ->
      tax + total * Map.get(@tax_rates, category, 0.0)
    end)
  end
end
EOF

args.command:
cd /workspace && python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re

path = Path('lib/invoice_pipeline/rules.ex')
text = path.read_text()

values_pattern = re.compile(r'(?m)^(?P<indent>\s*)categories\s*=\s*category_totals\([^\n]+\)\n(?P=indent)subtotal\s*=.*\n(?P=indent)discount\s*=.*\n(?P=indent)tax\s*=.*$')

def rounded_values(match):
    indent = match.group('indent')
    categories_line = match.group(0).splitlines()[0]
    return '\n'.join([
        categories_line,
        f'{indent}subtotal = money(Enum.sum(Map.values(categories)))',
        f'{indent}discount = money(discount_for(subtotal))',
        f'{indent}tax = money(tax_for(categories))',
    ])

text, values_count = values_pattern.subn(rounded_values, text, count=1)
text, total_count = re.subn(r'(?m)^(\s*)total:\s*.*$', r'\1total: money(subtotal - discount + tax),', text, count=1)

if 'defp money(' not in text:
    index = text.rfind('\nend')
    if index == -1:
        raise SystemExit('rules.ex module end not found')
    text = text[:index] + '\n\n  defp money(value), do: Float.round(value + 1.0e-9, 2)' + text[index:]

if values_count != 1 or total_count != 1:
    raise SystemExit('rules.ex did not contain the expected implemented summarize block and total field')

path.write_text(text)
PY
mix format lib/invoice_pipeline/rules.ex
